labels.name: duplicate
labels.description: This issue or pull request already exists

labels.name: wontfix
labels.description: This will not be worked on

issues.title: Add reference implementation, test vectors
issues.author: cathieyun
issues.body:
To facilitate adoption, it would be helpful to add a reference implementation, which produces test vectors. 
The P384 ciphersuite as suggested in the spec would be sufficient for now.

issues.title: Isolate `Scalar` structure from `Group` class
issues.author: mmaker
issues.body: Currently `Scalar` methods are inside `Group`, which does not allow for an appropriate Fiat-Shamir transform. There should be a separate `ScalarField` type attribute inside `Group`, performing the scalar operations.

issues.title: Publish proof-of-concept implementation
issues.author: mmaker
issues.body:
An ideal proof-of-concept implementation should contain 3 examples: 
- DLEQ proofs
- proofs of ElGamal encryption
- Schnorr proof of knowledge of a secret key

issues.title: Create test vectors for implementation
issues.author: mmaker
issues.body: Create example test vectors for the examples in the implementation.

issues.title: Isolate commitment serialization from prove_batchable
issues.author: mmaker
issues.body:
The method `prove_batchable` and `verify_batchable` should be agnostic wrt the sigma protocol and the choice of the hash function. 
In order for this to happen, commitment and response serialization must be deferred to a separate procedure in SigmaProtocol.
Therefore:  move serialization to `serialize_commitment` and `serialize_response`.

issues.title: Support for short proofs
issues.author: mmaker
issues.body:
Right now the non-interactive sigma protocol proving function outputs batchable proofs `(commitment, response)` 
which (I think) require point validation and are generally larger than short proofs of the form `(challenge, response)`.

This issue is to track the addition of a `short_prove` and `short_verify function`

issues.title: Support messages as part of the zk proof
issues.author: mmaker
issues.body:
it is typical in signatures of knoweldge and sometimes in zk proofs to add a string label to a zkp, which is plugged into the fiat-shamir transformation. After a chat with trevor i have the impression that for him the way to go would be 

```
challenge = SHAKE128(description || message || commitment)
```

so i think we should find a way to add the message to the domain separator, if needed.

issues.title: Support deterministic nonce generation
issues.author: mmaker
issues.body:
It is good practice in the signature world to re-seed the operating system randomness with the signing key. One such example is [RFC6979](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6979). This requires some care because (1) we want to preprocess the information with the challenge generation, (2) we don't want to expose the witness to side-channel attacks, and (3) make the spec more complicated than it needs to be. 

For the case of sigma protocols, something simple as 
```
nonce     = SHAKE128(description || message || random || PAD || witness)
challenge = SHAKE128(description || message || commitment)
```

would be ideal, but the problem comes in when thinking of multi-round protocols, each of which might requires extra randomness in the middle of the protocol. 

The approach used in [Nimue](https://github.com/mmaker/nimue) is to have the NARG prover state also hold a private sponge, which is ratcheted seeded with operating system randomness every time an absorb is performed. This seems OK but wasteful.
A better alternative would be to expect the interactive (ZK) prover to take as input an rng, and seed that rng with the witness and some os randomness once and for all. The downside of this approach is that recovering the initial seed will break the whole protocol, but this does not seem to be such a problem? all in all, there is not much academic literature about leakage in zk proof of these sort.

pulls.title: Isolate Scalar struct from Group
pulls.author: ChihChengLiang
pulls.body:
We attempt to fix #2 with this PR.

Test
```
cd poc
make
sage test_zkp.sage 
```

comments.author: mmaker
comments.body:
nice job, but there's still some things that are not handled: 

- `hash_to_scalar` is part of the description https://github.com/ChihChengLiang/spfs/blob/scalar-struct/poc/groups.sage#L190  
- the change needs to propagate to the elliptic curve implementations
In particular, https://github.com/ChihChengLiang/spfs/blob/scalar-struct/poc/groups.sage#L246 scalar serialization for NIST curves is still part of `NISTGroup`. 
Same for https://github.com/ChihChengLiang/spfs/blob/scalar-struct/poc/groups.sage#L287.

reviews.author: ChihChengLiang
reviews.body: I commented on the decision to use composition (wrapping GF) rather than inheritance (subclass GF)

comments.body:
Eventually I gave up the inheritance approach due to the complexity.
GF is not a simple class. It is created using a [UniqueFactory](https://doc.sagemath.org/html/en/reference//structure/sage/structure/factory.html#sage.structure.factory.UniqueFactory). To initialize it properly, we need __new__ instead of __init__, which causing a series of new complexity.
We use the `__getattr__` to get all the GF properties and methods.
